title: Track-Evolution Detrended Pace-Change Features
summary: Pirelli explicitly notes that abrasiveness, expected temperatures, and especially track evolution change tyre behavior over a weekend, while F1’s official tyre-performance graphics rely on relative tyre performance from live timing and stint context.
rationale: Pirelli explicitly notes that abrasiveness, expected temperatures, and especially track evolution change tyre behavior over a weekend, while F1’s official tyre-performance graphics rely on relative tyre performance from live timing and stint context. The current pipeline uses coarse medians, but not same-lap detrending that separates true tyre drop-off from global track-speed changes.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year`, compute causal same-lap baselines such as `LapNumber x Compound`, `LapNumber x Is_Slick`, and full-field lap medians/ranks. Add residuals like driver lap-time delta versus same-lap compound median, versus own 3-lap EWM, and second-difference curvature in those residuals. Back off hierarchically when subgroup counts are small.
expected_effect: Should sharpen the signal for genuine in-lap degradation cliffs or crossover behavior that precedes a stop, while reducing noise from rubbering-in, ambient changes, and circuit-specific pace drift.
risk: Wet/intermediate subgroups can be thin, so same-lap residual features may become unstable unless they back off to broader baselines.
